task_completion.description: The agent successfully loaded the abdominal CT scan (Patient 5) into 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible with a volume loaded. The slice views show abdominal anatomy (liver, spine, ribs).

task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the axial slice level showing the portal vein bifurcation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Red (axial) slice view shows the liver with the main portal vein splitting into left and right branches. This is the standard level for C/RL measurement.

task_completion.description: The agent placed a measurement ruler across the Caudate Lobe.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ruler/markup line is visible on the Caudate Lobe. The Caudate Lobe is the distinct anatomical region located posteriorly between the IVC (large circular vein near spine) and the ligamentum venosum/portal vein. The line should measure the transverse width.

task_completion.description: The agent placed a measurement ruler across the Right Lobe of the liver.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ruler/markup line is visible extending from the lateral edge of the Caudate Lobe (where the previous measurement ended) to the far right lateral margin of the liver.

task_completion.description: The agent calculated the C/RL ratio and saved the final JSON report with classification.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent is seen saving a file named 'crl_ratio_report.json' or displaying its content. The content should include 'caudate_width_mm', 'rightlobe_width_mm', 'crl_ratio', and a classification (e.g., 'Normal' or 'Suggestive of cirrhosis').

integrity.description: The agent used the 3D Slicer GUI Markups/Ruler tool to perform measurements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Markups module active or the mouse interacting with the slice view to place ruler points. The agent did not simply guess numbers.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI to write the output files directly via terminal commands without measuring.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen using `echo` or `cat` to create the measurement files with hardcoded numbers before performing the visual task.

integrity.description: The measurement lines are placed on the liver anatomy, not in empty space or on the wrong organ.
integrity.visual_evidence: The ruler lines are superimposed on the liver tissue in the axial view.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses Patient 5 from the 3D-IRCADb-01 dataset. The correct measurement level is the axial slice at the portal vein bifurcation. The Caudate Lobe is located between the IVC (posterior) and the ligamentum venosum (anterior). The Right Lobe width is measured from the lateral edge of the caudate to the right liver margin. Tolerances for automated scoring are: Caudate width +/- 8.0mm, Right Lobe width +/- 15.0mm, Slice level +/- 20.0mm. The threshold for cirrhosis classification is a ratio >= 0.65.
